claims.claim: Padded boundary linears integrate into full modern-decoder generation; pads never enter RMSNorm / RoPE / SwiGLU / softmax cores.
claims.safe_wording: Padded full-generation correctness verified at float64 precision under CPU local emulation.

claims.claim: Low-interaction operator-compatible path achieves exact generation with one TEE-accelerator round trip per decode step under exact_visible_attention.
claims.safe_wording: Exact low-interaction mode: greedy match 1.0, online_boundary_round_trips_per_decode_step = 1, intermediate_tee_reentry = false.
claims.unsafe_wording: Exact low-interaction mode hides attention maps.

claims.claim: RoPE-safe pre-mask mode eliminates transient plain Q/K/V on the accelerator: qkv_projection_outputs_masked_directly = true.
claims.safe_wording: Per-head block-diagonal RoPE-plane rotation masks commute with apply_rope and are folded into the qkv-projection, yielding masked Q_tilde / K_tilde / V_tilde directly.

claims.claim: Token/chunk norm-mask granularity disrupts the full-sequence Gram-matrix leakage that sequence-shared Q exhibits; row L2 norms remain preserved by RMSNorm correctness.
claims.safe_wording: Token-wise masking preserves per-row L2 norms (required by RMSNorm) and disrupts off-diagonal Gram; chunk(k) preserves within-chunk Gram and disrupts cross-chunk Gram.

claims.claim: Attention maps are hidden from the accelerator transcript ONLY in trusted_softmax_attention mode; exact_visible_attention exposes them by construction.
claims.remaining_blocker: Real TEE with low boundary latency.
claims.safe_wording: Attention hiding requires trusted/secure softmax or approximate attention. Exact low-interaction with accelerator-side softmax exposes the QK invariant.
claims.unsafe_wording: Row-wise score shifts provide attention privacy.

claims.claim: exact_visible_attention mode preserves the QK invariant by construction; attention scores and probabilities are visible on the accelerator.
claims.safe_wording: Exact low-interaction baseline trades attention-map privacy for one-round-trip exactness.

claims.claim: Dense V x V LM-head orthogonal mask is not feasible for real LLM vocab sizes; permutation and block-diagonal masks scale but disclose multiset/block leakage.
claims.safe_wording: Dense N_vocab not scalable to V >= 16k; permutation (O(V) storage) and block (O(V b) storage) are scalable alternatives with explicit leakage notes.

claims.claim: LoRA adapters integrate with the Stage 7.6h main protocol for q/k/v/o/up/gate/down_proj insertion sites; rank padding hides true rank but padded rank is observable.
claims.safe_wording: Forward LoRA padded-boundary identity holds at every supported site at float64; A_tilde = M^{-1} A R, B_tilde = R^{-1} B N_out.

claims.claim: The per-(session, layer, head) masked KV invariant holds under a CPU synthetic paged cache with block-table remapping.
claims.safe_wording: Block-table indexing preserves K_tilde = K @ N_K and V_tilde = V @ N_V per session; cross-session block sharing disabled by default.

claims.claim: Per-session masks (Q_l, N_K, N_V, N_vocab) are independent; same prompt under two sessions produces different masked boundary fingerprints; cross-session prefix sharing off by default.
claims.safe_wording: Per-session orthogonal masks are sampled independently; boundary fingerprints differ across sessions for the same prompt.

claims.claim: Active-adversary integrity is supported ONLY as a probabilistic spot-check prototype; no verifiable computation.
claims.safe_wording: Detection rate scales with checked_fraction; no false alarms under correct execution; not a verifiable computation primitive.

claims.claim: No real GPU or TEE wall-clock latency / throughput is measured; all numbers are FLOP / byte estimates or CPU emulated counts.
claims.safe_wording: Complexity-model evidence only; no real wall-clock.

claims.claim: No formal cryptographic / semantic / differential-privacy security is claimed.
claims.remaining_blocker: Cryptographic protocol design or formal proof out of scope for this project.
claims.safe_wording: Algebraic correctness + leakage / cost accounting only.

claims.claim: No full Qwen / LLaMA deployment unless a real wrapper exists; only synthetic tiny modern decoder is exercised.
claims.safe_wording: Tiny modern decoder used as paper-ready surrogate; scaling to LLaMA / Qwen requires the corresponding tokenizer + model loader + GPU kernels.
claims.unsafe_wording: Qwen / LLaMA deployed in TEE-GPU split.

claims.claim: Hardware side channels (timing, memory, power, RDMA) are NOT evaluated.
claims.safe_wording: Side-channel evaluation out of scope; would require real hardware platform and counter-measure design.

claims.claim: Sliding window attention is supported under the Stage 7.6g/h/i masked invariants in a CPU synthetic abstraction.
claims.safe_wording: Within the active window, the QK invariant and rolling KV invariant hold at float64 precision; eviction policy is the public window size.

claims.claim: The rolling-window KV cache obeys K_tilde_window = K_plain_window @ N_K per (layer, head).
claims.safe_wording: Per-(layer, head) masked invariant preserved over the rolling window; old tokens evicted by the public window policy.

claims.claim: fp16 / bf16 / int8 / int4 are SIMULATED on CPU; no real GPU kernels or quantized models are measured.
claims.safe_wording: CPU-simulated precision sweep; provides error bounds for the protocol's algebraic recovery, not real hardware performance.
claims.unsafe_wording: Real GPU fp16 / bf16 / int8 / int4 performance.

claims.claim: Well-conditioned mask families (orthogonal, permutation, RoPE-plane block rotation, block-diagonal) are recommended for low-precision deployment; ill-conditioned dense masks amplify error proportionally to the condition number.
claims.safe_wording: Condition-number sweep shows error scales with cond(M); orthogonal masks remain at machine epsilon, dense ill-conditioned masks amplify error.
claims.unsafe_wording: Any mask family is fine for fp16.

claims.claim: Generation processors (temperature, top-k, top-p, repetition penalty, stop tokens, bad words, forced tokens) are safe ONLY when executed inside the trusted side, AFTER logits recovery.
claims.safe_wording: Main theorem: if recovered logits equal plain logits, every standard logit processor produces identical output under the same trusted randomness.
claims.unsafe_wording: Bad word list cryptographically hidden from accelerator.

claims.claim: Output length / stop timing is observable unless the trusted side separately pads or batches to hide it.
claims.remaining_blocker: Explicit length-hiding policy with padded generation.
claims.safe_wording: Output length is a side-channel that is NOT addressed by the current protocol; it must be hidden by additional padding or batching policy.

claims.claim: Standard 1D RoPE (and RoPE scaling) is covered ONLY when the rotation planes are unchanged; M-RoPE and multi-axis positional encodings are NOT covered.
claims.safe_wording: RoPE-plane block-diagonal mask commutes with apply_rope iff B_K is the same 2D rotation in each RoPE pair.

claims.claim: M-RoPE / multimodal positional encoding is NOT covered.
claims.safe_wording: Future work.

claims.claim: MoE router / expert dispatch is NOT covered.
claims.remaining_blocker: Trusted routing or masked expert dispatch.
claims.safe_wording: Future work.

claims.claim: Speculative decoding (draft / target verification) is NOT covered.
claims.remaining_blocker: Speculative-decode threat model + masked draft model.
claims.safe_wording: Future work.

claims.claim: Quantized real-model deployment is NOT supported unless a real GPU quantized backend exists.
claims.safe_wording: CPU-simulated quantization only; real deployment requires GPU int8 / int4 kernels and weight loaders.

limitations: Audit table is paper-ready guidance, not a formal proof.
Evidence-artifact existence is checked; semantic validity is not re-evaluated here.
Unsupported claims must be NEVER written as supported in the paper.
paper_safe_wording: All paper claims are classified into supported, proxy_supported, cost_model_only, or unsupported, with the corresponding safe and unsafe wording per claim.
unsafe_wording_to_avoid: Combining algebraic correctness with cryptographic security in the abstract.
